Name: kernel_optimization_for_inference
Title: Optimizing Kernel-Level Throughput for Machine Learning Model Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing kernel-level throughput using techniques such as kernel fusion, register blocking, and memory coalescing, we can achieve significant improvements in machine learning model inference speed on commodity GPUs.
Related Work: Recent research has focused on optimizing machine learning model inference using various techniques such as quantization, sparsity, and layer-skipping. However, these approaches often overlook the importance of kernel-level optimization. Our proposal builds upon existing work by emphasizing the importance of optimizing kernel-level throughput, as seen in studies such as AMP-ViT and SNIP.
Abstract: This research proposal aims to optimize kernel-level throughput for machine learning model inference on commodity GPUs. We will investigate the use of kernel fusion, register blocking, and memory coalescing to improve inference speed. Our approach will emphasize empirical evaluation and measurement of wall-clock latency and tokens-per-second on real hardware, ensuring that our results are reproducible and relevant to real-world applications.
Experiments: We will conduct a series of experiments to evaluate the effectiveness of our optimization techniques, including: (1) optimizing kernel-level throughput for popular machine learning models, (2) measuring the impact of kernel fusion, register blocking, and memory coalescing on inference speed, and (3) evaluating the benefits of our optimized kernels on commodity GPUs. We will use a combination of profiling tools, such as nvprof and Nsight, to collect detailed measurements of kernel performance and memory bandwidth.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The main risk factors and limitations of our proposal include: (1) the complexity of optimizing kernel-level throughput, which may require significant expertise and resources, and (2) the potential for limited improvements in inference speed due to hardware constraints.
